qa_method.name: GCN over QA reachability graph
qa_method.description: Two-layer plain-torch GCN trained transductively on the full mod-24 (b,e) grid (576 nodes). Node features are the QA full packet (b, e, d, a, C, F, G, phi_b, phi_e) where phi_b = b mod (m//3), phi_e = e mod (m//3). Edges are produced by applying the QA generators sigma, mu, lambda_2, nu (tools.qa_ml.qa_generators) to every node and recording the (src, dst) pairs where the generator is defined. Adjacency is symmetrized and normalized as D^(-1/2) (A + I) D^(-1/2) (Kipf-Welling). Singularity (24, 24) is included in the graph but masked out of supervision and evaluation.
qa_method.observer_projection: Integer (b, e) discrete states -> integer feature packet -> float64 normalized adjacency -> torch GCN (float-side observer) -> orbit-class logits. Theorem NT boundary crossed once on input and once on output; no float feedback into the QA layer.

datasets.name: qa_orbit_mod24_full_grid
datasets.source: tools.qa_ml.build_dataset(24) -- 576 (b,e) pairs; class counts cosmos=567 satellite=8 singularity=1 (singularity masked from supervision); ground truth qa_orbit_rules.orbit_family

calibration_provenance.learned_on: qa_full feature transform is deterministic; GCN weights initialized from torch default with fixed seed; adjacency is precomputed once
calibration_provenance.procedure: no calibration step beyond per-fold z-score standardization of node features (mean/std fit on train mask only)
calibration_provenance.domain_of_origin: self-contained mod-24 grid; no inheritance from another domain

diffs: first QA-ML graph benchmark; no prior framework being ported
ground-truth labels from canonical qa_orbit_rules; generators sigma/mu/lambda_2/nu mirror qa_structural_algebra naming

source_mapping.primary_source: 0 errors against simulation for mod-9 and mod-24
source_mapping.mapping_rationale: QA-ML v1 (orbit classifier sample-efficiency benchmark) showed that polynomial QA expansion alone is weak and that orbit learnability is controlled by modular phase. v2 tests whether the QA reachability graph -- whose edges are exactly the canonical generators sigma, mu, lambda_2, nu -- carries enough topological signal to lift orbit prediction further. A GCN's message-passing has access to neighbor labels through the same algebraic moves that define the orbit families, so this is a direct test of whether QA generator topology (not just QA features) is informative for ML.

ablation.destroyed_structure: Replace the normalized QA-generator adjacency with the identity matrix; same node features, same model architecture, same seed. This collapses the GCN to a 2-layer MLP on qa_full features and isolates the contribution of the reachability graph.
ablation.expected_direction: with_graph should outperform without_graph at small n_train because message passing along sigma/mu propagates labels within the satellite cluster and lambda_2/nu edges expose the modular phase structure that single-node MLPs can only learn from many examples.
